pr_title: TEST-#5897: Add more robust tests for numpy API
body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?
This PR adds tests for several previously uncovered test cases in the numpy API.
<details>
<summary>New test cases:</summary>
- Error cases for improper broadcast dimensions
- Broadcasting with an `out` array specified
- Basic behavior for `__setitem__` and `_set_shape`
- Binary operations with a scalar argument
- remainder, mod, argmax, and argmin basic behavior
- shape, transpose, split, hstack, and append basic behavior
</details>

This PR also includes a few minor bugfixes related to broadcasting issues (I did not file issues for those), and adds xfail'd tests for the following:
- #5831
- #5886
- #5893
- #5894
- #5896

-->

## What do these changes do?

<!-- Please give a short brief about these changes. -->

Using `other_name` option looks like a complication. In all cases where `broadcast_apply` function is used, it is assumed that the user-defined function has the following signature: `func(df, other)` (only the name of `other` parameter changes on different calls). Nothing prevents us from passing this parameter as a positional one.

pr_title: FEAT-#5816: Implement '.split' method for axis partitions
body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

The PR introduces an implementation of the `.split()` method for axis partitions, making it possible to avoid materialization of the virtual row partitions during reshuffling.

ASV results:
```
MODIN_TEST_DATASET_SIZE="Big" asv continuous origin/master HEAD --launch-method=spawn -b TimeSortValues --no-only-changed -a repeat=5

All benchmarks:

       before           after         ratio
     [cd7611cd]       [9316fbcc]
     <master>       <issue_5816>
       949±50ms         910±50ms     0.96  benchmarks.TimeSortValues.time_sort_values([1000000, 10], 10, False)
       947±40ms         898±20ms     0.95  benchmarks.TimeSortValues.time_sort_values([1000000, 10], 1, False)
       1.03±0.04s         909±60ms    ~0.89  benchmarks.TimeSortValues.time_sort_values([1000000, 10], 2, False)
```

pr_title: TEST-#0000: use custom Distributed
body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

<!-- Please give a short brief about these changes. -->

In the custom version of this `Distributed`, we remove the call of `self._get_computation_code()`, which is called in `submit->_graph_to_futures`. `_get_computation_code` takes about 75% of the total call time of `submit` function and it looks like it's just collecting additional information that might not be collected in a non-debug mode. With this change, Dask tests are faster than Ray tests. It is interesting to try to propose to implement a mechanism that disables the call to this function in `Distributed` library itself.

pr_title: TEST-#2544: delay `codecov` notifications until all reports have been sent
body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

<!-- Please give a short brief about these changes. -->

* The main change is that all coverage reports are first sent to a temporary storage location using `actions/download-artifact@v3.0.2`. After all the reports have been received, we combine them, convert them to `xml` format and send them in the same way as before using `codecov/codecov-action@v3`.
* Also, `fail_ci_if_error: true` new option  is now used, very often reports were not loaded for some reason, now, if such a situation occurs, it will be easy to restart `upload-coverage` job. It's much faster than rerunning tests.
* This is a step forward to https://github.com/modin-project/modin/issues/5122.

## What do these changes do?

<!-- Please give a short brief about these changes. -->

The purpose of PR is to move forward with https://github.com/modin-project/modin/issues/5122.

Specifically, this pull request tries to solve two problems:

pr_title: PERF-#5740: allow `read_csv`, `read_fwf`, `read_table`, `read_custom_text` functions be executed fully asynchronous; introduce `ModinDtypes`
body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

* The PR introduce new type that can be stored in `dataframe._dtypes`: `callable() -> pandas.Series`. Together with [PR#5677](https://github.com/modin-project/modin/pull/5677), it makes the execution of `read_csv` function completely asynchronous. Unlike an index, a `dtypes` can be restored from internal partitions, however, if zero pickling does not work, this will lead to additional copy operations.
* As with the index cache in [PR#5677](https://github.com/modin-project/modin/pull/5677), this PR offers additional functions to make working with the dtypes cache easier: `has_dtypes_cache`, `copy_dtypes_cache`.
* For backends that do not support this new behavior, it is enough to trigger the `dtypes` calculation in the constructor, as is done for hdk.
* First side effect of this pull request is that `._dtypes` field is now always copied if possible. Same reasoning as for the index cache.
* Second side effect is if reading occurs in a context that deletes the files from which we are reading, then in some situations, given the asynchronous behavior, this will lead to the fact that by the time reading actually starts, the file may already be deleted. This, for example, affects our tests, which had to be rewritten.
* Also, one of the debatable changes in the PR is the removal of the section of code that performs `astype` function in the case of categorical data types. Turns out we don't have tests for that. However, [the reproducer](https://github.com/modin-project/modin/issues/1165) that was used for this change no longer works, and the error is not reproduced. Based on this, I decided to delete this section. Obviously, this is not a sufficient condition for deleting this section, only a necessary one. However, if we really need this code, then it is not clear why it is called only after the read functions, and not after all. As far as I understand, we are not sure that for one column we will have the same type among all partitions.

pr_title: PERF-#5674: reduce data transferring in `_launch_tasks` function
body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

<!-- Please give a short brief about these changes. -->

The main idea behind this pull request is to reduce the amount of data that needs to be serialized. For this, the following is done:
* `cls.parse` function is cached (only serialized on first use).
* Most of the dictionary data that we pass to remote functions (for `read_csv` function) is the same across partitions, allowing us to serialize it once.
* There is a problem in the serialization of dictionaries by Dask, whose behavior is different from other engines. If a dictionary is passed, then the dask serializes only the values, and the keys are used for service purposes. As far as I understood, there is a problem very similar to a key collision: https://github.com/dask/distributed/issues/3965#issuecomment-659422586. However, a simple workaround - wrapping the dictionary in a new type (UserDict) - circumvents this problem, and, as shown [here](https://github.com/modin-project/modin/pull/5678/files#r1126411771), does not slow down the execution.

Some thoughts:
* When passing references to remote functions, we need to pass them as **positional** parameters so that the engines can automatically deserialize them (at least this is true for Ray, but for unification, I want to do the same for all engines). This requires a change in the signatures of methods `_launch_tasks`, `parse` and `single_worker_read`.
* I guess one of the debatable parts here is that not all `parse` functions have the same signature (for other read functions). If these changes are made now, they will be unused, which I do not really like.

Perf results:
* On one of the workloads, where there is an intensive reading of many files, quite small ones. I'm seeing a speedup of `_launch_tasks` function by about 15 seconds (from ~18 sec down to ~3 sec).

pr_title: PERF-#5675: make index calculation for `read_csv` function lazy; introduce `ModinIndex`
body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

<!-- Please give a short brief about these changes. -->

* The PR introduce new type that can be stored in `dataframe._index_cache`: `callable() -> (pandas.Index, list)`. This allows the index to be computed lazily when it cannot be restored from internal partitions. This functionality is useful at least for Modin's IO functions in cases where sequential reading from several files occurs. However, for the full effect, it is also necessary to defer the evaluation of dtypes, this is the task of a separate pull request.
* We are starting to use this opportunity already in this pull request for the `read_csv` function. I suggest extending the application to other reading functions after this functionality has been tested for `read_csv`.
* Storing different types in this `._index_cache` complicates its use. A more complicated condition is needed to make a copy of this field, and it also precludes the possibility of using this field instead of an index directly if this field is not None. To level these effects, it is proposed to introduce new functions through which interaction will take place: `has_index_cache`, `copy_index_cache` (also `has_columns_cache`, `copy_columns_cache` to be aligned, even if we don't allow new type for `._columns_cache`)
* For backends that do not support this new behavior, it is enough to trigger the index calculation in the constructor, as is done for `hdk`.
* Another side effect of this pull request is that `._index_cache` and `._columns_cache` fields are now always copied. This change should introduce some slowdown, but in principle it should be done, otherwise bugs will occur. However, I believe that a new pandas feature can help here - copy on write (CoW), but I don't know if it will be implemented for `Index.copy()`.
* A new callable type for `_columns_cache` can be easily added, but I don't want to do this until there is code that will use this feature.

pr_title: FEAT-#5666: Support logic operations on modin numpy arrays
body:
## What do these changes do? This implements the following numpy [array logic operations](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/routines.logic.html) in modin:
List of functions 
- all
- any
- isfinite
- isinf
- isnan
- isnat
- isneginf
- isposinf
- iscomplex
- isreal
- isscalar
- logical_not
- logical_and
- logical_or
- logical_xor
- greater
- greater_equal
- less
- less_equal
- equal
- not_equal
`logical_and`, `or`, and `xor` currently do not support 2D to 1D broadcasts; the logic for these seems to be slightly different from the existing arithmetic binary operators, as attempting to use `Binary.register(lambda df, r, *args, **kwargs: pd.DataFrame(np.logical_and(df, r)))` yields the following error: `NotImplementedError: Cannot apply ufunc to mixed DataFrame and Series inputs.` This can be addressed either in forthcoming refactors of Modin's numpy internals, or I can look further into it and try to introduce a fix in this PR as well. - [x] first commit message and PR title follow format outlined [here](https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html#commit-message-formatting) > **_NOTE:_** If you edit the PR title to match this format, you need to add another commit (even if it's empty) or amend your last commit for the CI job that checks the PR title to pick up the new PR title. - [x] passes `flake8 modin/ asv_bench/benchmarks scripts/doc_checker.py` - [x] passes `black --check modin/ asv_bench/benchmarks scripts/doc_checker.py` - [x] signed commit with `git commit -s` - [x] Resolves #5666 - [x] tests added and passing - [x] module layout described at `docs/development/architecture.rst` is up-to-date

pr_title: FIX-#5621: Do not preserve suboptimal partitioning on `keep_partitioning=False`
body:
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Chigarev <dmitry.chigarev@intel.com>

<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

This may increase data transfer between workers when `keep_partitioning=False`, however, don't we always want the data to be shuffled when this parameter is passed?

The problem is that `keep_partitioning=False` is the default one for `PartitionManager.map_axis_partition`, so it may affect cases where some code calls this method without explicitly specifying this argument:
https://github.com/modin-project/modin/blob/0df5fbf7a66663110878633467e4488ad37b0337/modin/core/dataframe/pandas/partitioning/partition_manager.py#L537
Most of the cases we explicitly specify `keep_partitioning=True`, there's only one case that use the default value for this parameter:
https://github.com/modin-project/modin/blob/0df5fbf7a66663110878633467e4488ad37b0337/modin/core/dataframe/pandas/dataframe/dataframe.py#L2812-L2816

pr_title: PERF-#5586: Precompute resulting '.merge' partitioning based on the arguments
body:
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Chigarev <dmitry.chigarev@intel.com>

<!--
Thank you for your contribution!
Please review the contributing docs: https://modin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/development/contributing.html
if you have questions about contributing.
-->

## What do these changes do?

The PR extends the capabilities of using the `keep_partitioning` parameter by replacing it with the `num_splits` parameter, allowing to extra-specify the desired number of splits for the result of a full-axis function.

This allows us to precompute and specify the desired partitioning of the `.merge()` and `.join()` results thus avoiding the generation of an excessive amount of partitions caused by [`split_result_of_axis_func(num_splits=NPartitions.get())`](https://github.com/modin-project/modin/blob/b8bfd7198f5f8b324065760cc44b4c40e35ad795/modin/core/dataframe/pandas/partitioning/axis_partition.py#L204)   most of which are empty ones.
